content:
Stonk Journal Pricing Overview

Stonk Journal costs exactly $0 per month. No hidden fees. No freemium model. No premium tiers. The entire platform is completely free, ad-free, and comes with no data monetization — a rarity in the trading journal space where most competitors either charge monthly subscriptions or plaster their interfaces with advertisements.

Founded in 2021, Stonk Journal has built its reputation on simplicity and transparency. While most trading journals use a freemium model to hook users before converting them to paid plans, Stonk Journal offers its full feature set at zero cost. This makes it the cheapest option available for traders looking to log, analyze, and improve their performance across stocks, options, futures, forex, and crypto markets.

For traders on tight budgets — especially beginners and side-hustlers — this pricing model is hard to beat. But does "free" automatically mean "best value"? Let's dig deeper.

---

All Stonk Journal Plans Compared

There is only one plan: Free, $0/month.

Feature Stonk Journal (Free)
Monthly Cost $0
Ads None
Trade Journaling ✓ Unlimited
Performance Analytics ✓ Win rate, profit factor, drawdown tracking
Risk/Reward Calculations ✓ Automatic R/R ratio computation
Trade Setups & Notes ✓ Full documentation support
Multiple Accounts/Portfolios ✓ Supported for strategy segregation
Multi-Asset Support ✓ Stocks, options, futures, forex, crypto
Broker Integration ✗ Manual entry only
Mobile App ✗ Not available
Advanced Analytics ✗ Limited vs. paid competitors
Data Monetization None — your data stays yours

That's it. One plan, zero cost, no upsell path. Stonk Journal's entire value proposition is built on the idea that traders shouldn't have to pay to learn from their mistakes.

---

Free Plan / Free Trial

Stonk Journal doesn't have a "free trial" because the entire product is free. There's no freemium paywall hiding advanced features behind a credit card requirement. You can access everything immediately upon signup, with zero commitment and no trial expiration.

What You Get:

  • Unlimited trade entries with full customization (entry/exit prices, dates, commissions)
  • Automatic Risk/Reward ratio calculation — essential for position sizing and risk management
  • Performance dashboard showing win rate, profit factor, average win/loss, and drawdown metrics
  • Multiple portfolio management — segregate strategies, assets, or time periods
  • Trade setup documentation — record your thesis, entry triggers, and exit conditions
  • Support for six asset classes in a single journal (stocks, options, futures, forex, crypto, and more)

What You Don't Get:

  • Broker integration: No automatic trade import from Interactive Brokers, TD Ameritrade, or other platforms. You'll manually enter every trade, which takes time but forces deliberate reflection.
  • Mobile app: Stonk Journal works in browsers only. If you need to log trades on your phone immediately after closing positions, you'll need a mobile-friendly web version or a different journal.
  • Advanced analytics: Compared to paid competitors like Tradervue, Stonk Journal's analytics are solid but not comprehensive. You won't find Monte Carlo simulations, equity curve analysis, or custom indicators.
  • Premium support: Community-based help only — no dedicated support team.

Is the free tier worth starting with? Absolutely. There's zero downside risk. If you outgrow Stonk Journal's features after six months, you can migrate to a paid platform with your historical data. In the meantime, you'll have built valuable journaling discipline and identified which advanced analytics actually matter to your edge.

---

Hidden Costs and Fees

This is where Stonk Journal shines: there are no hidden costs.

You won't encounter:

  • Data feed charges: Some trading platforms charge for real-time quotes. Stonk Journal doesn't monetize data feeds.
  • Premium features locked behind paywalls: No feature unlocking. No "upgrade to unlock advanced risk metrics" nonsense.
  • Freemium conversion traps: Unlike competitors, there's no dark pattern trying to convert you to a paid plan after 30 days.
  • Data monetization: Stonk Journal explicitly does not sell user data to third parties. Your trade history stays private.
  • Exchange fees: Not applicable — Stonk Journal is a journal, not a broker or market data provider.

Your only costs are indirect: the time investment in manual trade entry and the opportunity cost of potentially missing advanced features available in premium competitors.

---

Stonk Journal Pricing vs Competitors

Here's how Stonk Journal's pricing compares to leading alternatives in the trading journal space:

Platform Starting Price Best For
Stonk Journal $0/month Budget-conscious traders, multi-asset journaling, privacy-first approach
Tradervue ~$30/month (approx.)* Professional day traders needing advanced analytics, broker integration, community
eSpeed Trading Journal ~$15–$50/month (approx.)* Futures and day traders seeking detailed P&L breakdowns and chart overlays
Thinkorswim (TD Ameritrade) Built-in Journal Free (with account) Traders exclusively using TD Ameritrade who want integrated broker tools

*Approximate pricing based on public sources. Verify current pricing on competitor websites.

The Stonk Journal advantage: Zero cost. No commitment required. Full feature access immediately.

The trade-off: Manual data entry and limited advanced analytics compared to $30+/month competitors. For high-frequency traders or those managing millions in capital, paid platforms offer broker integration, real-time syncing, and statistical depth that Stonk Journal doesn't match. But for intermediate to advanced traders managing personal accounts, Stonk Journal's free tier may be all you need.

When to upgrade to a paid competitor:

  • You're trading daily (manual entry becomes a time burden)
  • You manage multiple brokerage accounts and need automatic syncing
  • You need Monte Carlo simulations or equity curve analysis to evaluate edge
  • You're serious enough to justify a monthly software subscription

---

Is Stonk Journal Worth the Price?

Short answer: Yes, for most traders. When something costs $0 and delivers core journaling functionality, the value proposition is hard to argue against.

For whom is Stonk Journal the best choice?

  • Beginners: You're learning to trade. Journaling discipline matters more than advanced analytics. Stonk Journal's clean interface forces you to reflect on each trade without overwhelming you with metrics. No cost means zero friction to starting.
  • Part-time traders: You're trading 1–5 times per week. Manual entry is manageable. You don't need real-time broker syncing because you're not managing high-frequency positions. Stonk Journal covers your use case perfectly.
  • Multi-asset traders: You juggle stocks, crypto, and options simultaneously. Most journals specialize in one asset class. Stonk Journal supports all six in one dashboard, saving you from managing multiple subscriptions.
  • Privacy-conscious traders: You don't want your trade data mined by third parties or sold to hedge funds. Stonk Journal's commitment to zero data monetization is rare and valuable.
  • Cost-conscious traders: Every dollar saved on tools is a dollar that compounds in your portfolio. If Stonk Journal meets your needs, why pay for premium?

For whom is Stonk Journal NOT the best choice?

  • Day traders: Manual entry for 20+ trades per day is unsustainable. You need automatic broker integration.
  • Professional quants: You need statistical analysis Stonk Journal doesn't offer: Monte Carlo simulations, portfolio heat maps, correlation analysis, and rolling Sharpe ratio calculations.
  • Mobile-first traders: Stonk Journal doesn't have a mobile app. If you log trades from your phone between meetings, this is a dealbreaker.
  • High-frequency options traders: Advanced Greeks analysis and volatility surface tracking aren't available. Platforms like Tradervue are built for this.

Value assessment: Stonk Journal delivers 80% of what a professional trader needs at 0% of the cost. That remaining 20% — broker integration, advanced analytics, mobile app — might be critical for your workflow. But for the majority of traders, it's overkill. Stonk Journal's honest strength is knowing its limits and not charging you for features you don't need.

---

How to Save on Stonk Journal

You're already saving the maximum: $0 per month. There are no discount codes, annual billing plans, or student discounts because there's no pricing to discount.

But here's how to maximize value from Stonk Journal:

  • Use multiple portfolios: Segregate strategies by asset class or time period. Instead of buying separate journals, use Stonk Journal's portfolio feature to run side-by-side experiments.
  • Combine with free data sources: Stonk Journal plays well with free market data from Yahoo Finance, Alpha Vantage, or your broker's API. No integration cost.
  • Export and analyze externally: Most trading journals allow CSV exports. Use Stonk Journal as your entry point, then pivot to Python/Excel for deeper analysis if needed. No additional cost.
  • Leverage the community: Stonk Journal has an active community forum. Free advice often beats paid support. Spend time in the community before upgrading to a paid competitor.

When to consider spending money: Only upgrade to a paid platform when Stonk Journal's constraints (no mobile, no broker integration, limited analytics) are genuinely blocking your ability to trade profitably. Treat paid tools as an investment only after validating your edge.

---

Bottom line: Stonk Journal is the honest choice for traders who want to build journaling discipline without financial pressure. Its $0 price tag is backed by genuine functionality: unlimited logging, solid analytics, multi-asset support, and privacy. Is it perfect? No. But it's free, it works, and it might be all you ever need. Start here before spending money elsewhere.

For more details, visit the Stonk Journal review or browse all trading journals for paid alternatives.
